content:
# 1 Peter Chapter 5 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph, 01), pages 268-269 | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/1PE_005_page_001.jpg, 1PE_005_page_002.jpg

1. [The] elders among you I exhort, the fellow-elder and witness of the sufferings of the Christos, the [one] also [being] a sharer of the glory about to be revealed:

2. Shepherd the flock of the God among you, overseeing not by compulsion but willingly according to God, not for shameful gain but eagerly,

3. not as lording over the allotted [ones], but becoming examples of the flock.

4. And when the chief Shepherd is manifested, you will receive the unfading crown of the glory.

5. Likewise, younger [ones], be subject to [the] elders. And all [of you], toward one another, clothe yourselves with humility, because "The God opposes [the] proud, but gives grace to [the] humble."

6. Be humbled therefore under the mighty hand of the God, that he may exalt you in [the] time,

7. having cast all the anxiety of you upon him, because it matters to him concerning you.

8. Be sober, be watchful. The adversary of you, [the] devil, as a roaring lion walks about, seeking whom to swallow up;

9. whom resist, firm in the faith, knowing the same [kinds] of sufferings are being accomplished upon the brotherhood of you in [the] world.

10. And the God of all grace, the [one] having called you into the eternal glory of him in Christos, [you] having suffered a little, he himself will restore, will establish, will strengthen, will ground [you].

11. To him [be] the dominion into the ages. Amen.

12. Through Silvanos, the faithful brother as I reckon, through few [words] I wrote to you, exhorting and testifying this to be [the] true grace of the God, into which stand.

13. She in Babylon, [the] co-chosen [one], greets you, and Markos the son of me.

14. Greet one another with a kiss of love. Peace to all of you, the [ones] in Christos.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

- **5:1** -- Sinaiticus reads ΣΥΝΠΡΕΣΒΥΤΕΡΟΣ (fellow-elder) with the unassimilated ΣΥΝ- prefix, characteristic of the scribe's spelling habits. The text also reads ΜΑΡΤΥΣ (witness) rather than a variant.
- **5:2** -- Sinaiticus includes ΕΠΙΣΚΟΠΟΥΝΤΕΣ (overseeing), which some manuscripts omit. The phrase ΚΑΤΑ ΘΕΟΝ (according to God) is present in Sinaiticus.
- **5:5** -- The quotation is from Proverbs 3:34 (LXX). Sinaiticus reads ΑΛΛΗΛΟΙΣ (toward one another) with the instruction to clothe with humility.
- **5:6** -- Sinaiticus reads ΕΝ ΚΑΙΡΩ (in [the] time) rather than ΕΝ ΚΑΙΡΩ ΕΠΙΣΚΟΠΗΣ (in [the] time of visitation).
- **5:8** -- Sinaiticus reads ΚΑΤΑΠΙΕΙΝ (to swallow up / to devour). The image of the lion is drawn from Psalm 22:13 and other OT imagery.
- **5:10** -- Sinaiticus reads four future-tense verbs: ΚΑΤΑΡΤΙΣΕΙ, ΣΤΗΡΙΞΕΙ, ΣΘΕΝΩΣΕΙ, ΘΕΜΕΛΙΩΣΕΙ (will restore, will establish, will strengthen, will ground). Some manuscripts read these as optatives (wishes).
- **5:11** -- Sinaiticus has the shorter doxology reading ΤΟ ΚΡΑΤΟΣ ΕΙΣ ΤΟΥΣ ΑΙΩΝΑΣ (the dominion into the ages), without the additional "of the ages" (τῶν αἰώνων) found in some later manuscripts.
- **5:12** -- Sinaiticus identifies ΣΙΛΟΥΑΝΟΥ (Silvanos) as the letter-carrier/amanuensis. The phrase ΔΙ ΟΛΙΓΩΝ (through few [words]) is a conventional epistolary formula.
- **5:13** -- "She in Babylon" (Η ΕΝ ΒΑΒΥΛΩΝΙ) -- the feminine article likely refers to the co-chosen church (ΕΚΚΛΗΣΙΑ being feminine), understood as the congregation in Rome (Babylon as cryptonym). ΜΑΡΚΟΣ Ο ΥΙΟΣ ΜΟΥ (Markos the son of me) designates a spiritual relationship.
- **5:14** -- Sinaiticus reads ΦΙΛΗΜΑΤΙ ΑΓΑΠΗΣ (kiss of love) rather than the Pauline ΦΙΛΗΜΑΤΙ ΑΓΙΩ (holy kiss). The letter closes with ΕΝ ΧΡΙΣΤΩ (in Christos) rather than ΕΝ ΧΡΙΣΤΩ ΙΗΣΟΥ (in Christos Yeshua), as found in some manuscripts.
- **Subscript** -- Following 5:14, the manuscript has ΠΕΤΡΟΥ Α (Petrou A, "Of Peter, 1") as the book subscript, visible at the bottom of column 1 on page 269.
